texto: People can go on telling themselves things like “Think before you speak!” or “You don’t believe that yourself” or “Forget it!” But they can also say, “Oh, what lovely flowers!” or “Are you a phone freak or something?” or “Suit yourself,” or “This record makes me feel so happy!’ Wonderful! Words are delightful little gifts we exchange like the Easter eggs we paint and hide for others to find and enjoy. I’d forgotten that, but you reminded me of it.
explicacao: Metáfora atribuída a Thomas Brussig: compara elementos do cotidiano para falar de vínculos afetivos e cuidado mútuo. O trecho “People can go on telling themselves things like “Think before you speak!” or “You don’t believe t…” usa imagem concreta para lembrar que reflexao, conversation, cute pedem movimento e interpretação pessoal, em tom de mudanca e cute.

frase_original: People can go on telling themselves things like “Think before you speak!” or “You don’t believe that yourself” or “Forget it!” But they can also say, “Oh, what lovely flowers!” or “Are you a phone freak or something?” or “Suit yourself,” or “This record makes me feel so happy!’ Wonderful! Words are delightful little gifts we exchange like the Easter eggs we paint and hide for others to find and enjoy. I’d forgotten that, but you reminded me of it.
